content.claim: Roma pupil segregation in Hungary is the second highest in the region, with 62% of Roma children aged 6-15 attending schools where all or most pupils are Roma
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Does not isolate contribution of 6/8-year tracks versus church-school admissions autonomy or geographic settlement patterns
Reflects systemic segregation but does not prove the extended gimnázium mechanism is primary driver
No longitudinal data on whether segregation rate changes with track availability
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
